text: The communication system shall assist operators and users in performing tasks by provision of services.
The communication system shall be based on open and widely accepted methods with a high degree of interface possibilities.
The communication system shall be robust and reliable.
Faults in the communication system shall not cause malfunction of an individual wind turbine.
The communication system shall be designed such that faults of a sub-system interferes as little as possible with functions of the communication system as a whole.
The condition monitoring system shall provide status and analysis reports for components.
The system shall make values available for display on operator HMI and for storage in databases.
The system shall allow updating of values to be selectable down to an interval of 1 second.
The plant controller shall store all data for transmission on demand.
The system shall control access to operational functions by a set of rules to restrict authenticated clients to pre-determined services and objects.
The system shall provide local or remote monitoring of the status and changes of states for operational devices.
The system shall allow an operator or automatic function to operate equipment via control functions.
The system shall generate an alarm when data takes a value representing an abnormal state.
The alarm management functions shall allow an operator to visualise and acknowledge alarms.
The system shall scan devices for alarms and operator control actions and record events chronologically with date and time information.
The system shall provide services to retrieve all parameter names and values or only those that differ from default values.
The system shall provide network management functions to identify communication objects and devices.
The system shall provide time synchronisation of devices within the communication system.
The system shall perform self-checking to detect if an object or device is operational.
The system shall support software management including version control and download.
The system shall allow an authorised operator to start and stop functions or objects within the system.
The communication system shall provide basic services for connection establishment and release.
The communication system shall provide authentication services.
The communication system shall provide identification of functional objects and devices.
The communication system shall provide reliable communication over a network.
The system shall support periodic data transfer.
The system shall support data transfer on demand.
The system shall support event driven spontaneous data transfer.
The system shall support command and set point transfer.
The system shall transmit alarm data from the wind turbine immediately upon occurrence.
The system shall time stamp all data using UTC time.
The accuracy and resolution of the timestamp shall be at least 10 ms.
The system shall provide signal quality and scanning rate properties for all analogue measured values.
The system shall allow grouping of analogue and binary values for reading in a single transaction.
The system shall provide server and client authentication.
The system shall deliver data from its originator to its destination with an acceptable residual error rate to ensure data integrity.
The system shall allow for encryption of transferred data items to ensure confidentiality.
The delay in execution of system management functions shall not exceed 2 seconds.
The overall transfer time for services in time critical functions shall not exceed 0.5 seconds.
The communication system shall allow for redundant communication channels.
The system shall include functionality to transfer stored data to central storage after restoration of communication.
The system shall manage naming of objects and variables in a hierarchical naming system.
All analogue process values shall be accessible in standard SI-units or other physical units.
The system shall require a confirmation for all set point updates.
The system shall store and display binary values at level shift with a corresponding date and time tag.
The system shall use a handshake procedure for commands that start or stop a mechanical component or change software.
The system shall store operational events in an event log for transmission on demand.
The plant controller shall provide an alarm log buffer depth of at least one year.
The plant controller shall provide an event log buffer depth of at least one year.
The system shall operate on TCP/IP standards and procedures.
